activities_projects.title: Timeline of the Bhakti Movement
activities_projects.objective: Understand the historical development of the Bhakti movement
steps: Research major Bhakti saints such as Ramanuja, Kabir, Mirabai, Tulsidas, and Chaitanya
Arrange them in chronological order
Create a timeline showing when and where they lived
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the historical timeline and spread of the Bhakti movement
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of timeline and clarity of presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Teachings Analysis Poster
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the teachings of Bhakti and Sufi saints
steps: Select two saints, one from Bhakti tradition and one from Sufi tradition
Write their major teachings and beliefs
Illustrate similarities and differences through diagrams or drawings
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops analytical understanding of devotional teachings
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of comparison and creativity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Devotional Literature Study
steps: Select a devotional poem by Kabir, Mirabai, or Guru Nanak
Read and interpret the poem
Write a short explanation of its message and theme
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops interpretation skills for historical literature
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of interpretation and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Bhakti and Sufi Map Project
activities_projects.objective: Understand geographical spread of devotional movements
steps: Mark important places associated with Bhakti and Sufi saints
Use different symbols for Bhakti and Sufi centers
Add a legend explaining the map symbols
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands regional spread and influence of devotional movements

activities_projects.title: Saint Biography Research
activities_projects.objective: Research the life and influence of a devotional saint
steps: Choose one saint such as Kabir, Guru Nanak, or Mirabai
Research their life, teachings, and followers
Write a one-page report and present it to the class
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness of research and clarity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Role Play on Bhakti Teachings
steps: Form small groups
Create a short scene depicting a teaching of Kabir or Mirabai
Perform the role play in front of the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Enhances understanding of teachings and teamwork
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of message and performance (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparison Chart: Bhakti vs Sufi Traditions
activities_projects.objective: Identify similarities and differences between traditions
steps: Create a table comparing beliefs, practices, and leaders
Include at least five comparison points
Write a short conclusion about their similarities
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops comparative understanding of devotional traditions
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and accuracy of comparison (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Devotional Music Exploration
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of music in devotional traditions
steps: Listen to examples of Bhajans or Qawwalis
Write the main theme of the song
Explain how music spreads devotional messages
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands cultural role of devotional music

activities_projects.title: Interview Activity – Religious Harmony
activities_projects.objective: Connect historical teachings with modern society
steps: Interview a family member or teacher about religious harmony
Ask how teachings of saints promote unity
Write a short report summarizing the discussion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates historical teachings to modern social values
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of reflection and clarity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Devotional Movement Quiz Preparation
steps: Prepare 15 questions based on the chapter
Include multiple choice and short answer questions
Conduct a quiz among classmates
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of questions and participation (10 marks)
